mission.title: distribution: notification endpoints cannot be configured with environment variables
mission.description: notification endpoints cannot be configured with environment variables. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify distribution troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your distribution deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution -n distribution --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review distribution configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution
```
We are using the notification API to build an index of images pushed for building a search API.

steps.title: Apply the fix for notification endpoints cannot be configured with…
steps.description:
Sorry about the confusion. A lot of the details of how this works are covered here: https://github.com/docker/distribution/blob/master/docs/configuration.md#override-specific-configuration-options.

I would suggest creating a configuration file and build it out from there. I usually test with
```yaml
notifications:
    endpoints:
        - name: pandora
          url: http://our-event-server.orgname.net/events
          timeout: 1s
          threshold: 10
          backoff: 1s
          disabled: false
```

steps.title: Upgrade distribution to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade distribution:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade distribution distribution/distribution --namespace distribution
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution
helm list -n distribution
```

steps.title: Confirm notification endpoints cannot be configured with… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution -n distribution --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Sorry about the confusion. A lot of the details of how this works are covered here: https://github.com/docker/distribution/blob/master/docs/configuration.md#override-specific-configuration-options.

I would suggest creating a configuration file and build it out from there.
codeSnippets:
notifications:
    endpoints:
        - name: pandora
          url: http://our-event-server.orgname.net/events
          timeout: 1s
          threshold: 10
          backoff: 1s
          disabled: false

version: 0.1
log:
  level: debug
  fields:
    service: registry
    environment: development
storage:
  filesystem:
    rootdirectory: /usr/local/var/lib/registry
http:
  addr: :5000
  debug:
      addr: localhost:5001
  headers:
      X-Content-Type-Options: [nosniff]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with distribution installed or the issue environment reproducible.
